Biography
Biography
Lindsay Jamieson is a teaching professor and associate dean of teaching faculty at the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University. Her area of research focuses on algorithms and theory. Additionally, Jamieson is the chair of ACM-W North America, which supports, celebrates, and advocates internationally for the full engagement of women in all aspects of the computing field.
Jamieson earned her doctorate in computer science from Clemson University and her bachelor’s in computer science from DePauw University. Prior to joining Northeastern in 2021, she was an associate professor of computer science at St. Mary’s College of Maryland.
